summary: Extracted MarmotContext.leaveGroup's inline body into a Deps-injected leaveGroupImpl.ts (mirroring grantAdminImpl.ts, AC-BOUND-1). The extracted impl re-derives last-member from a fresh getGroupMembers(mlsGroup.state) read on every invocation and gates both the kind-13 sendRumorSafe call and the kind-9 mlsGroup.sendApplicationRumor call on that check, fixing the pre-existing bug where a solo-group leave sent both rumors unconditionally. Added clearInviteLinksForGroup to inviteLinkStorage.ts (mirroring clearPendingJoinRequestsForGroup) and wired both leak-fixing clears into the purge sequence, unconditionally, on both the abandon and normal-leave paths.
design_decisions: leaveGroupImpl's signature is (deps: Deps, groupId: string, selfPubkeyHex: string): Promise<boolean> — selfPubkeyHex stays a positional argument rather than a Deps field, mirroring grantAdminImpl's (deps, groupId, targetPubkey) shape; the per-call subject is not conflated with the impl's side-effecting dependencies.

MarmotGroupLike in leaveGroupImpl.ts carries only { state: any; sendApplicationRumor: (rumor: any) => Promise<unknown> } — the minimal shape the impl actually calls methods on (state for getGroupMembers, sendApplicationRumor for the kind-9 send). sendRumorSafe's own group-shaped argument is typed via the same MarmotGroupLike, not marmot-ts's real MarmotGroup type, to keep the impl's only coupling to the real type at the MarmotContext wiring boundary.

The send-gate condition is `mlsGroup && selfPubkeyHex && !lastMember`, computed after an unconditional `const members = mlsGroup ? deps.getGroupMembers(mlsGroup.state) : undefined` and `const lastMember = isLastMember(members, selfPubkeyHex)` — exactly the shape specified in the story brief, so isLastMember's fail-closed-to-false behavior (undefined members) resolves to 'not last member' regardless of whether mlsGroup itself is present. When mlsGroup is absent, no send is attempted anyway (nothing to call methods on) — R2's 'send anyway' framing describes the logical resolution of the last-member question, not a literal send with no group reference.

isLastMember is imported directly from leaveEligibility.ts (R1) — not reimplemented inline, preserving the own-pubkey verification and fail-closed contract that a bare `members.length === 1` check would drop.

sendRumorSafe and buildRumor are injected as Deps fields, passed as closures at the MarmotContext.leaveGroup call site (`sendRumorSafe: (group, rumor) => sendRumorSafe(group as any, rumor as any)`, `buildRumor` passed directly since its signature already matches). Neither function was exported from MarmotContext.tsx.

The two new purge clears (clearPendingJoinRequestsForGroup, clearInviteLinksForGroup) are appended after clearUnreadGroup and before reloadGroups/markBackupDirty, inside the same unconditional (post-send-block) sequence as the pre-existing purge steps — not conditioned on which send branch fired.

clearInviteLinksForGroup mirrors clearPendingJoinRequestsForGroup's exact shape (entries() -> filter groupId -> Promise.all(del(nonce))) rather than reusing loadInviteLinks + a loop, keeping the two per-group clears structurally identical for future maintainers.

verification_answers.answer: git diff --stat -- app/src/lib/marmot/inviteLinkStorage.ts app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx app/tests/unit/marmot/inviteLinkStorage.test.ts shows exactly those three tracked files changed, plus two new untracked files (app/src/lib/marmot/leaveGroupImpl.ts, app/tests/unit/marmot/leaveGroupImpl.test.ts) confirmed via git status --short. No file outside this list was touched by this story. Reading the MarmotContext.tsx diff directly: the leaveGroup useCallback body is fully replaced by a thin wrapper (four dynamic imports + one leaveGroupImpl(deps, groupId, selfPubkeyHex) call) — no send or purge logic remains inline. The diff's only other hunk is the getLiveMemberPubkeys interface/implementation, which was S2's addition and predates this story (confirmed against S2's result.json, which lists it as S2's change); this story's diff does not touch it.

leaveGroupImpl.ts has zero top-level @internet-privacy/marmot-ts imports (getGroupMembers arrives as a Deps field), so leaveGroupImpl.test.ts needs no vi.mock('@internet-privacy/marmot-ts') — confirmed by inspecting both files directly. Deps is declared as a `type` (not `interface`). MarmotGroupLike's `state` field is typed `any` (not `unknown`), matching cancelInvitationImpl.ts/grantAdminImpl.ts's stated contravariance-avoidance rationale. MarmotGroupLike is declared fresh in leaveGroupImpl.ts, not imported from grantAdminImpl.ts or cancelInvitationImpl.ts — duplicated verbatim per the established convention.

grep -n 'sendRumorSafe\|buildRumor' app/src/context/MarmotContext.tsx confirms both remain module-private (declared as bare `function`/`async function`, never `export`ed) — unchanged from before this story. leaveGroupImpl.ts declares both as Deps fields (sendRumorSafe: (group: MarmotGroupLike, rumor: any) => Promise<void>; buildRumor: (kind, content, pubkey, tags?) => any) with zero import of either name. MarmotContext.leaveGroup's call site passes `sendRumorSafe: (group, rumor) => sendRumorSafe(group as any, rumor as any)` and `buildRumor` (passed directly, signature-compatible) as object-literal Deps fields at the await-import call site — the same pattern grantAdmin/renameGroup use for Proposals.

leaveGroupImpl.ts's Deps type lists all 15 fields: getGroup, getGroupMembers, sendRumorSafe, buildRumor, removeGroupFromStorage, clearMemberProfiles, clearMessages, clearPollData, clearGroupMedia, clearProfileRequestMemos, clearUnreadGroup, clearPendingJoinRequestsForGroup, clearInviteLinksForGroup, reloadGroups, markBackupDirty. All nine sizing-note names (removeGroupFromStorage, clearMemberProfiles, clearMessages, clearPollData, clearGroupMedia, clearProfileRequestMemos, clearUnreadGroup, reloadGroups, markBackupDirty) are present as Deps fields; grep -n 'import' app/src/lib/marmot/leaveGroupImpl.ts shows only two imports (isLastMember, LEAVE_INTENT_KIND+serialiseLeaveIntent, both pure lib/marmot/ modules) — none of the nine names is a direct top-level import.

leaveGroupImpl computes `const members = mlsGroup ? deps.getGroupMembers(mlsGroup.state) : undefined;` unconditionally at the top of every call, from the mlsGroup just fetched via `await deps.getGroup(groupId)` in that same invocation — never a parameter, never cached across calls (leaveGroupImpl has no retry loop, unlike grantAdminImpl, so there is no cross-attempt caching concern within a single call; each MarmotContext.leaveGroup invocation calls leaveGroupImpl exactly once, and each call performs its own getGroup + getGroupMembers pair). Confirmed by the AC-STRUCT-3 unit test asserting getGroupMembers was called exactly once, with the exact mlsGroup.state object.

leaveGroupImpl.test.ts's 'AC-SEND-1: solo group (last member) — neither kind-13 nor kind-9 is sent' test configures makeDeps({ members: [SELF] }) (a solo-group live read) and asserts both deps.sendRumorSafe and the mlsGroup mock's sendApplicationRumor are .not.toHaveBeenCalled(). This fails on pre-story code (no leaveGroupImpl module exists) and would also have failed against the pre-extraction inline body, which sent unconditionally whenever mlsGroup && selfPubkeyHex with no last-member check at all.

leaveGroupImpl.test.ts's 'AC-SEND-2: two-member group — both kind-13 and kind-9 are sent exactly once' test uses a genuinely distinct fixture (makeDeps({ members: [SELF, OTHER] })) from AC-SEND-1's, asserting deps.sendRumorSafe and the sendApplicationRumor mock are each .toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1), and additionally checks the sent rumors' kind fields (13 and 9 respectively) to confirm the right rumor reached each mechanism. The two tests are independent — deleting either leaves the other's branch unverified.

Two separate tests — 'AC-PURGE-1: solo/abandon path clears pending join requests and invite links' (makeDeps({ members: [SELF] })) and 'AC-PURGE-1: multi-member/normal path clears pending join requests and invite links' (makeDeps({ members: [SELF, OTHER] })) — each assert deps.clearPendingJoinRequestsForGroup and deps.clearInviteLinksForGroup were called with the correct groupId. Both pass, confirming the purge calls are unconditional on the send branch: the implementation places them after the send-gate block, in the same flat sequence as the pre-existing purge steps.

inviteLinkStorage.test.ts's new 'clearInviteLinksForGroup' describe block, following the joinRequestStorage.test.ts:113-130 template literally: (a) 'removes all links for the specified group, leaving other groups intact' seeds group-1 (n1, n3) and group-2 (n2), clears group-1, asserts n1/n3 gone and n2 intact; (b) 'is a no-op for a group with no links' seeds group-1 (n1), clears a nonexistent group, asserts n1 untouched; (c) 'is a safe no-op when called again on an already-cleared group' clears group-1 twice in a row, asserting no throw and n1 stays gone. All three pass.

leaveGroupImpl's signature is (deps: Deps, groupId: string, selfPubkeyHex: string) — no memberPubkeys, isLastMember, or similar precomputed-boolean parameter exists. Every last-member check traces to the single `getGroupMembers(mlsGroup.state)` call made at the top of the function body, using the mlsGroup fetched by that same call's `await deps.getGroup(groupId)` (cross-checked against VQ-S3-005 — same call, same invocation, no caching). MarmotContext.leaveGroup's wrapper does not read S2's getLiveMemberPubkeys or thread anything from LeaveGroupButton's modal-open read into leaveGroupImpl's Deps or arguments — the two live reads (S2's context method, S3's impl-internal read) remain structurally independent, sharing no call path or cached value.

grep -nE "from ['\"](@/src/context|\.\./\.\./context|\.\./context)" app/src/lib/marmot/leaveGroupImpl.ts returns zero matches (empty output, exit code 1). sendRumorSafe and buildRumor remain unexported in MarmotContext.tsx (grep -n 'export.*sendRumorSafe\|export.*buildRumor' MarmotContext.tsx returns zero matches) and arrive in leaveGroupImpl.ts exclusively as Deps fields, per VQ-S3-003 — no import-back occurred.

testing.unit: make test-unit: 173 test files, 3350 passed, 1 skipped (pre-existing, unrelated) — no regressions. New: leaveGroupImpl.test.ts (9 tests, all passing). Extended: inviteLinkStorage.test.ts (14 -> 17 tests, +3 for clearInviteLinksForGroup, all passing).
testing.e2e: Not run — out of scope for S3 per the story brief (S4 owns AC-FLOW-1/2/3).

retrospective.reason: clean run, no friction worth recording — grantAdminImpl.ts and its test file provided a complete, unambiguous template for the Deps-injection pattern, the wiring, and the makeDeps test factory shape.
